symbols.name: m
symbols.def: number of latent confounders

symbols.name: n
symbols.def: n=m+2

symbols.name: L
symbols.def: truncation endpoint

symbols.name: r,a
symbols.def: cumulant order and number of Y arguments

symbols.name: i,ell,j
symbols.def: latent-loading and source indices

symbols.name: P
symbols.def: observational law on R^2

symbols.name: X
symbols.def: first coordinate under P

symbols.name: Y
symbols.def: second coordinate under P

symbols.name: S_j
symbols.def: centered source indexed by j

symbols.name: gamma
symbols.def: forward direct-effect slope

symbols.name: rho_i
symbols.def: forward latent-loading slope indexed by i

symbols.name: delta
symbols.def: reverse direct-effect slope

symbols.name: sigma_i
symbols.def: reverse latent-loading slope indexed by i

symbols.name: u_j
symbols.def: u_0=(1,gamma), u_j=(1,rho_j) for 1<=j<=m, and u_{m+1}=(0,1)

symbols.name: v_j
symbols.def: v_0=(1,0), v_j=(sigma_j,1) for 1<=j<=m, and v_{m+1}=(delta,1)

symbols.name: c_{jr},d_{jr}
symbols.def: r-th source cumulants in the forward and reverse parameterizations

symbols.name: kappa_{r,a}(P)
symbols.def: Cum_P(X repeated r-a times,Y repeated a times)

symbols.name: T_L(P),t
symbols.def: T_L(P)=(kappa_{r,a}(P):2<=r<=L,0<=a<=r) in lexicographic order, and t is a vector in its complexification

symbols.name: Theta^right_{m,L},Theta^left_{m,L},theta,eta
symbols.def: Theta^right_{m,L}=C^{m+1}xC^{n(L-1)} and Theta^left_{m,L}=C^{m+1}xC^{n(L-1)}, with coordinates theta=(gamma,rho,c) and eta=(delta,sigma,d), respectively
symbols.role: complexified structural parameter domains and their elements

symbols.name: Theta^{right,circ}_{m,L},Theta^{left,circ}_{m,L}
symbols.def: the subsets of the corresponding Theta spaces with nonzero direct slope, distinct non-axis loading directions, and c_{jr}!=0 or d_{jr}!=0 for every 0<=j<=m+1 and 2<=r<=L

symbols.name: Phi^right_{m,L},Phi^left_{m,L}
symbols.def: axis-conditioned simultaneous binary-form maps from the corresponding Theta spaces to C^{q_L}

symbols.name: C^right_{m,L},C^left_{m,L}
symbols.def: Zariski closures of the images of Phi^right_{m,L} and Phi^left_{m,L} in C^{q_L}

symbols.name: R^right_{m,L}(t),R^left_{m,L}(t)
symbols.def: full complex fibers of Phi^right_{m,L} and Phi^left_{m,L} over t

symbols.name: G_m,pi,b
symbols.type: finite group, group element, and arrow index
symbols.space: G_m=Sym({1,...,m}), pi in G_m, b in {right,left}
symbols.def: G_m permutes the m latent-confounder indices, pi is an admissible source swap, and b indexes an arrow parameterization; the G_m action simultaneously relabels (rho_i,c_{ir}) or (sigma_i,d_{ir}) while fixing indices 0 and m+1

symbols.name: E_m,barE_m,H^right_m,H^left_m
symbols.def: E_m is the generic full-fiber opposite-arrow compatibility set, barE_m is its Zariski closure, and H^b_m is the generic parameter-level preimage of E_m on arrow b

symbols.name: F^right_{m,L},F^left_{m,L}
symbols.def: real loading-and-cumulant lists realizable by centered independent non-Gaussian sources with finite L moments in the forward and reverse parameterizations

symbols.name: K^star(m)
symbols.def: least L for which the arrow is generically identifiable over the real feasible forward and reverse parameter regions from T_L, with genericity defined by deletion of a proper real algebraic subset and with every feasible opposite-arrow fiber considered

symbols.name: S_m
symbols.def: selector based on nonemptiness of the two full feasible opposite-arrow fiber correspondences at order K

symbols.name: M,P_M,D(M),M^{sep}_{m,K}
symbols.type: structural model, law, direction functional, and model domain
symbols.def: M is a particular bivariate LvLiNGAM representation with its sources and directed edge, P_M is its observational law, D(M) is its directed edge, and M^{sep}_{m,K} is the nonzero-edge real-feasible structural-model domain on which P_M has no feasible representation with the opposite arrow through order K
symbols.role: well-posed causal target and separated domain

assumptions.condition: S_0 perp S_1 perp ... perp S_{m+1}
for every 0<=j<=m+1, S_j is not Gaussian

standard.name: bivariate linear acyclic model with latent confounders

definitions.name: Forward bivariate LvLiNGAM class
definitions.construction: {P in Laws(R^2) : there exist centered S_0,...,S_{m+1} such that ass:independent-sources, ass:finite-cumulants, ass:source-nongaussianity, ass:forward-axis-model, ass:forward-noncollinearity, and ass:forward-nonzero-edge hold}

definitions.name: Reverse bivariate LvLiNGAM class
definitions.construction: {P in Laws(R^2) : there exist centered S_0,...,S_{m+1} such that ass:independent-sources, ass:finite-cumulants, ass:source-nongaussianity, ass:reverse-axis-model, ass:reverse-noncollinearity, and ass:reverse-nonzero-edge hold}

definitions.name: Stacked joint-cumulant truncation
definitions.construction: T_L(P)=(kappa_{r,a}(P):2<=r<=L,0<=a<=r), where kappa_{r,a}(P)=Cum_P(X repeated r-a times,Y repeated a times) and q_L=L(L+3)/2-2.

definitions.name: Forward simultaneous binary-form map
definitions.construction: [Phi^right_{m,L}(gamma,rho,c)]_{r,a}=sum_{j=0}^{m+1}c_{jr}(u_{j1})^(r-a)(u_{j2})^a for 2<=r<=L and 0<=a<=r, with u_0=(1,gamma), u_j=(1,rho_j) for 1<=j<=m, and u_{m+1}=(0,1).

definitions.name: Reverse simultaneous binary-form map
definitions.construction: [Phi^left_{m,L}(delta,sigma,d)]_{r,a}=sum_{j=0}^{m+1}d_{jr}(v_{j1})^(r-a)(v_{j2})^a for 2<=r<=L and 0<=a<=r, with v_0=(1,0), v_j=(sigma_j,1) for 1<=j<=m, and v_{m+1}=(delta,1).

definitions.name: Generic retained-cumulant parameter loci
definitions.construction: Theta^{right,circ}_{m,L}={(gamma,rho,c) in Theta^right_{m,L}: gamma product_{i=1}^m(gamma-rho_i) product_{1<=i<ell<=m}(rho_i-rho_ell) product_{j=0}^{m+1}product_{r=2}^L c_{jr} !=0}; Theta^{left,circ}_{m,L}={(delta,sigma,d) in Theta^left_{m,L}: delta product_{i=1}^m(delta-sigma_i) product_{1<=i<ell<=m}(sigma_i-sigma_ell) product_{j=0}^{m+1}product_{r=2}^L d_{jr} !=0}.

definitions.name: Full axis-conditioned decomposition correspondences
definitions.construction: R^right_{m,L}(t)={theta in Theta^right_{m,L}: Phi^right_{m,L}(theta)=t} and R^left_{m,L}(t)={eta in Theta^left_{m,L}: Phi^left_{m,L}(eta)=t}.

definitions.name: Admissible source swaps
definitions.construction: For pi in G_m, pi.(gamma,(rho_i)_{i=1}^m,(c_{jr})_{j,r})=(gamma,(rho_{pi(i)})_{i=1}^m,(c_{pi(i),r})_{i=1}^m,c_{0r},c_{m+1,r}); the left action replaces rho,c,gamma by sigma,d,delta. Indices 0 and m+1 are fixed.

definitions.name: Generic full-fiber compatibility locus
definitions.construction: E_m={t in C^{q_K}: [R^right_{m,K}(t) intersects Theta^{right,circ}_{m,K} is nonempty and R^left_{m,K}(t) is nonempty] or [R^left_{m,K}(t) intersects Theta^{left,circ}_{m,K} is nonempty and R^right_{m,K}(t) is nonempty]}; barE_m=ZariskiClosure(E_m); H^right_m=Theta^{right,circ}_{m,K} intersects (Phi^right_{m,K})^{-1}(E_m) and H^left_m=Theta^{left,circ}_{m,K} intersects (Phi^left_{m,K})^{-1}(E_m).

definitions.name: Real moment-feasible parameter regions
definitions.construction: F^right_{m,L}={(gamma,rho,c) in R^{m+1} x R^{n(L-1)}: gamma!=0, rho_1,...,rho_m,gamma are pairwise distinct, and for every 0<=j<=m+1 there exists a centered non-Gaussian real S_j with E|S_j|^L<infty and Cum(S_j repeated r times)=c_{jr} for every 2<=r<=L}; F^left_{m,L} is the analogous set with delta!=0 and (delta,sigma,d).

definitions.name: Generic real information order
definitions.construction: K^star(m)=min{L>=2: for b in {right,left}, outside a proper real algebraic subset of F^b_{m,L}, the value Phi^b_{m,L}(theta) has no representation in F^{opposite(b)}_{m,L}}; set K^star(m)=infinity if the set is empty.

definitions.name: Separate-fiber direction selector
definitions.construction: S_m(t)=X->Y when R^right_{m,K}(t) intersects F^right_{m,K} is nonempty and R^left_{m,K}(t) intersects F^left_{m,K} is empty; S_m(t)=Y->X when the reverse condition holds; S_m(t) is undefined otherwise.

definitions.name: Separated nonzero-edge structural-model domain
definitions.construction: M^{sep}_{m,K}={M: M has a forward representation with theta in F^right_{m,K} and R^left_{m,K}(T_K(P_M)) intersects F^left_{m,K} is empty, or M has a reverse representation with eta in F^left_{m,K} and R^right_{m,K}(T_K(P_M)) intersects F^right_{m,K} is empty}.

definitions.name: Worked compatibility systems
definitions.construction: For m=1 and K=4, A_1 is the 12-equation incidence system t_{r,a}=c_{0r}gamma^a+c_{1r}rho^a+c_{2r}1{a=r}=d_{0r}1{a=0}+d_{1r}sigma^(r-a)+d_{2r}delta^(r-a), for r=2,3,4 and 0<=a<=r, with the generic-locus disjunction; its common-axis subfamily is rho=sigma=0, delta gamma=1, d_{0r}=c_{1r}, d_{1r}=c_{2r}, d_{2r}=c_{0r}gamma^r. For m=2 and K=6, A_2 is the 25-equation system t_{r,a}=c_{0r}gamma^a+c_{1r}rho_1^a+c_{2r}rho_2^a+c_{3r}1{a=r}=d_{0r}1{a=0}+d_{1r}sigma_1^(r-a)+d_{2r}sigma_2^(r-a)+d_{3r}delta^(r-a), for r=2,...,6 and 0<=a<=r, with the generic-locus disjunction; its common-axis subfamily is rho_1=sigma_1=0, delta gamma=1, sigma_2 rho_2=1, d_{0r}=c_{1r}, d_{1r}=c_{3r}, d_{2r}=c_{2r}rho_2^r, d_{3r}=c_{0r}gamma^r.

definitions.name: Separation handle
definitions.construction: Compare the simultaneous binary-form decompositions Phi^right_{m,K}(theta)=Phi^left_{m,K}(eta) after quotienting each same-arrow fiber by G_m, using the axis directions fixed in the two maps and the Jacobian ranks of the resulting quotient-fiber equations.

definitions.name: Real lower-order twin construction handle
definitions.construction: Solve the two axis-conditioned Waring systems through K_-=2m+1 over distinct real loading directions, then realize the resulting finite source-cumulant lists by compactly supported non-Gaussian laws through a truncated-moment-matrix perturbation.

definitions.name: Real exceptional-locus atlas handle
definitions.construction: Put d=m+1, K=2d, and n=d+1. Convert each source cumulant list k=(k_2,...,k_K) to raw moments mu_r=B_r(0,k_2,...,k_r), and impose the finite atomic certificate Q_K(k): there exist w_h>=0,z_h in R (1<=h<=n) with sum_h w_h=1, sum_h w_h z_h^r=mu_r for 1<=r<=K, and mu_2>0. Compute defining equations J_m for barE_m by saturating the simultaneous equations Phi^right(theta)=t=Phi^left(eta) separately by the right and left genericity products, eliminating the parameter variables, and intersecting the two resulting t-ideals. Form Gamma_b={(t,lambda):J_m(t)=0, Phi^b(lambda)=t, the b-loading inequalities hold, and Q_K holds for every source list}. Run a simultaneous sign-invariant cylindrical algebraic decomposition, using coefficients, discriminants, and principal subresultants, with t ordered before lambda and atomic witnesses. The induced t-cells stratify barE_m(R); label each cell by whether its right and left Gamma_b stacks are nonempty, and output every selected section/sector stack as the local description of R^b_{m,K}(t) intersect F^b_{m,K}.

statements.statement: For each pi in G_m, Phi^right_{m,L}(pi.theta)=Phi^right_{m,L}(theta) and Phi^left_{m,L}(pi.eta)=Phi^left_{m,L}(eta). If a forward structural model M has real-feasible parameter theta, relabelling its latent sources by pi gives a forward structural model with the same observational law P_M and D(M)=X->Y; the analogous reverse statement has D(M)=Y->X. Hence quotienting a same-arrow fiber by G_m identifies only source labels and never identifies opposite arrows.
statements.justification: The quotient used in the decomposition comparison is meaningful only if its allowed source relabelings do not erase the structural causal-direction target.
statements.gap: The source-label indeterminacy is implicit in higher-cumulant decompositions used by ChenPengHuangEtAl2025Direction and SchkodaRobevaDrton2024; neither states the bivariate direction-preservation property required for a quotient-fiber orientation claim.
statements.consumer: The lemma licenses the separation question to treat G_m-orbits as one within-arrow decomposition without laundering an opposite-arrow representation.

statements.statement: For every m>=1, does there exist a Zariski-open dense subset of each Theta^{right,circ}_{m,K} and Theta^{left,circ}_{m,K} whose images have no full opposite-arrow fiber, while each same-arrow fiber is a single G_m-orbit, and do these subsets meet F^right_{m,K} and F^left_{m,K} in nonempty relatively Euclidean-open sets? A positive answer gives generic real direction recovery from T_K modulo admissible source swaps.
statements.justification: This is the required sufficiency half of a one-order reduction, stated against every full opposite-arrow fiber rather than a reduced or relabeled stand-in.
statements.gap: ChenPengHuangEtAl2025Direction, Theorems 4.4-4.7, uses a k=m+2 cumulant matrix with entries through order 2m+3. This question asks whether the complete truncation through order 2m+2 strictly reduces that sufficient order through a simultaneous-decomposition argument; the parameter count q_K-p_K=m is only a necessary dimensional check, not a proof of separation.
statements.consumer: A positive resolution supplies a lower-order population orientation functional for the pairwise source-finding step of SchkodaRobevaDrton2024.

statements.statement: For every m>=1, is barE_m of codimension m in each C^right_{m,K} and C^left_{m,K}, and are H^right_m and H^left_m exactly the respective generic parameter points that retain a full opposite-arrow representation? Can the resulting full-fiber equations be made explicit for m=1 at K=4 and m=2 at K=6?
statements.justification: The question distinguishes genuine generic non-identification from a rank witness failing and states the exact parameter-level locus through the appropriate cumulant-map preimages.
statements.gap: Neither ChenPengHuangEtAl2025Direction nor ChenHuangCaiEtAl2024 describes the arbitrary-m full-fiber opposite-arrow compatibility locus, its codimension, or the fourth- and sixth-order instances.
statements.consumer: The equations define the null boundary for the margin-restricted finite-sample orientation-testing problem left open by Genin2021StatisticalUndecidability under latent confounding.

statements.statement: Is K^star(m)=2m+2 for every m>=1? This requires both a positive resolution of oeq:generic-separation at K=2m+2 and a Euclidean-open family of real forward and reverse laws in the stated classes with equal T_{K_-} at K_-=2m+1.
statements.justification: A dimension balance cannot pin a minimal order; the lower-order failure must be witnessed by real, moment-feasible opposite-arrow laws.
statements.gap: ChenPengHuangEtAl2025Direction supplies a sufficient rank construction but neither a full-truncation separation proof nor an open real reverse-twin family through order 2m+1. The prior eid_lnsem_cumulant_id record explains why failure of a particular rank witness is not an information-order lower bound.
statements.consumer: A positive resolution rules out every direction functional measurable from the joint cumulants through order 2m+1 on an open real model region.

statements.statement: Can one derive a finite semialgebraic stratification of barE_m(R) that determines exactly which full-fiber compatibility points have independent real non-Gaussian finite-K source representations on both arrows and gives explicit local descriptions of the two feasible fiber correspondences?
statements.justification: Complex compatibility does not by itself determine the real moment-feasible geometry relevant to applied orientation.
statements.gap: SchkodaRobevaDrton2024 establishes generic cumulant-rank mechanisms for multivariate discovery, not a real semialgebraic atlas for a bivariate full-fiber overlap; Genin2021StatisticalUndecidability establishes that asymptotic orientation need not yield unrestricted finite-sample decidability, but neither identifies this locus nor proves that a separation restriction is necessary.
statements.consumer: The atlas supplies a real-feasibility diagnostic for deciding when the separation-margin restriction required by the finite-sample orientation-testing problem highlighted by Genin2021StatisticalUndecidability is credible.

sampling_model.design: population identification from observational law P
sampling_model.units: independent draws of (X,Y) when estimation is later added
sampling_model.treatment: none; the target is causal direction
sampling_model.nuisance_estimation: not part of this exact-identification core

tldr: The project asks whether the least generic real cumulant order for orienting a separated nonzero-edge bivariate LvLiNGAM with m latent confounders is 2m+2 rather than the order 2m+3 used by Chen et al.'s rank matrix. The central sufficiency question compares the two complete simultaneous binary-form fibers at order 2m+2, quotients only latent-confounder label swaps that preserve the structural arrow, and tests against every opposite-arrow fiber. The exceptional set is a generic full-fiber compatibility locus, defined by fibers rather than an elimination-ideal shortcut: it retains a possible degenerate opposite-arrow representation while requiring the displayed representation to have nonzero retained cumulants. A separate real reverse-twin construction at order 2m+1 is required to turn the candidate order into a minimality result. The structural arrow is recovered as an observed-law functional only on the separated domain.

project_justification.gap: Chen et al. provide an order-2m+3 sufficient rank test, but not a full-fiber analysis of the order-2m+2 truncation, a direction-preserving treatment of within-arrow source swaps, an exact generic compatibility locus, or a real lower-order reverse-twin construction.
project_justification.niche: The fixed bivariate problem has simultaneous binary-form geometry across cumulant orders that is lost when one studies a single rank matrix, and it separates source-label ambiguity from structural arrow ambiguity.
project_justification.fill: The project studies generic full-fiber separation at order 2m+2 on a separated nonzero-edge structural-model domain, the codimension and equations of its image- and parameter-level exceptional loci, and the real order-2m+1 reverse-twin construction needed to certify the information-order frontier.

related_work: ChenPengHuangEtAl2025Direction is the direct comparator: Theorems 4.4-4.7 use a k=m+2 cumulant matrix whose entries reach order 2m+3; Open-ended Question 1 asks whether the full truncation through order 2m+2 strictly lowers that sufficient order, while Open-ended Question 3 supplies the necessary lower-order failure for a minimality conclusion. ChenHuangCaiEtAl2024 is the one-latent cumulant-asymmetry prototype and informs the m=1 fourth-order specialization, but is not an arbitrary-m order-frontier comparator. CaiHuangChenEtAl2023 develops recursive recovery under a One-Latent-Component condition rather than fixed-pair order calibration. SchkodaRobevaDrton2024 supplies the closest higher-cumulant rank and fiber-analysis ancestor, but studies multivariate discovery and compatible effect sets rather than the bivariate full-fiber overlap. TramontanoKivvaSalehkaleybarDrtonKiyavash2025, Section 3 and Theorem 3.1, uses Schkoda et al.'s finite-root recovery for a specified two-observed-variable graph and adds a proxy or instrument to identify a causal-effect root; it does not compare unrestricted full opposite-arrow fibers or determine the 2m+2 order frontier. CaiGaoHara2025Direction permits dependent latent-variable DAGs for full-DAG recovery and therefore informs, but does not resolve, the independent-source bivariate frontier. CaiHara2026SparsestLvLiNGAM addresses finite-sample sparsest-DAG recovery rather than the least population cumulant truncation for one arrow. Genin2021StatisticalUndecidability establishes that asymptotic orientation can fail to be unrestrictedly statistically decidable under latent confounding; it neither identifies the real compatibility locus nor proves that this project's separation restriction is necessary.
honest_scope: The standing targets are generic population orientation on the separated nonzero-edge independent non-Gaussian finite-moment bivariate LvLiNGAM domain and its full-fiber generic compatibility locus. The exact real information-order frontier, the real feasible atlas, finite-sample inference on a restricted domain, dependent-latent robustness, and direct-effect-coefficient identification remain open. The dimension difference q_K-p_K=m is a necessary check only; it is not a minimality certificate.

bibliography.citation: Wei Chen, Linjun Peng, Zhiyi Huang, Haoyue Dai, Zhifeng Hao, Ruichu Cai, and Kun Zhang (2025), Identification of Causal Direction under an Arbitrary Number of Latent Confounders, arXiv:2510.22711.
Wei Chen, Zhiyi Huang, Ruichu Cai, Zhifeng Hao, and Kun Zhang (2024), Identification of Causal Structure with Latent Variables Based on Higher Order Cumulants, AAAI 38, 20353-20361.
Ruichu Cai, Zhiyi Huang, Wei Chen, Zhifeng Hao, and Kun Zhang (2023), Causal Discovery with Latent Confounders Based on Higher-Order Cumulants, ICML.
Daniela Schkoda, Elina Robeva, and Mathias Drton (2024), Causal Discovery of Linear Non-Gaussian Causal Models with Unobserved Confounding, arXiv:2408.04907.
Daniele Tramontano, Yaroslav Kivva, Saber Salehkaleybar, Mathias Drton, and Negar Kiyavash (2025), Causal Effect Identification in lvLiNGAM from Higher-Order Cumulants, ICML 2025, arXiv:2506.05202.
Ming Cai, Penggang Gao, and Hisayuki Hara (2025), Causal Discovery for Linear DAGs with Dependent Latent Variables via Higher-order Cumulants, arXiv:2510.14780.
Ming Cai and Hisayuki Hara (2026), Learning Sparsest Linear Causal DAGs with Latent Confounders via Higher-Order Cumulants, arXiv:2607.05984.
Konstantin Genin (2021), Statistical Undecidability in Linear, Non-Gaussian Causal Models in the Presence of Latent Confounders, NeurIPS 34.

comparator_promise_table.comparator_claim: Theorems 4.4-4.7 identify direction with a k=m+2 cumulant matrix whose entries reach cumulant order 2m+3.
A higher-cumulant asymmetry criterion provides an early latent-variable causal-direction prototype.
Higher cumulants support recursive causal discovery under a One-Latent-Component condition.
Higher-order cumulant rank matrices support recursive latent-confounded discovery and finite compatible effect sets.

For specified proxy and underspecified-IV lvLiNGAM graphs, finite higher-order cumulants identify causal effects by refining the finite root set recovered for a two-observed-variable latent-confounded graph.
Higher-order cumulants identify full linear DAGs while allowing dependent latent variables.
A finite-sample higher-cumulant method recovers the sparsest linear causal DAG with an arbitrary number of latent confounders.
Unrestricted finite-sample orientation is statistically undecidable under latent confounding.

literature_checklist.author: Chen et al.
literature_checklist.one_line: Arbitrary-latent bivariate orientation by an order-(2m+3) cumulant-matrix rank criterion.

literature_checklist.author: Cai et al.
literature_checklist.one_line: Recursive higher-cumulant discovery under a One-Latent-Component condition.

literature_checklist.author: Schkoda, Robeva, and Drton
literature_checklist.one_line: Higher-order cumulant rank methods for latent-confounded LiNGAM discovery.

literature_checklist.author: Tramontano et al.
literature_checklist.one_line: Specified proxy and underspecified-IV graphs use finite cumulants to select causal-effect roots, not to settle bivariate opposite-arrow compatibility or a minimal truncation order.

literature_checklist.author: Cai, Gao, and Hara
literature_checklist.one_line: Higher-cumulant full-DAG discovery with dependent latent variables rather than a bivariate independent-source order frontier.

literature_checklist.author: Cai and Hara
literature_checklist.one_line: Finite-sample sparsest-DAG recovery under arbitrary latent confounding without a least-order bivariate population characterization.

literature_checklist.author: Genin
literature_checklist.one_line: Latent-confounded LiNGAM orientation lacks unrestricted finite-sample decidability.
